jokes.text: Well, did you ever have one of those mornings? You know, the kind when a big steel ball comes crashing through your apartment?
jokes.setup: Classic 'one of those mornings' setup implies minor inconveniences
jokes.punchline: Subverted by the absurdly extreme reality of a wrecking ball
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of a familiar phrase with perfectly absurd escalation. Jim's oblivious delivery makes it character-specific.

jokes.text: Well, you put up with a few inconveniences when you live in a condemned building.
jokes.setup: Shock that building was torn down without warning
jokes.punchline: Jim's casual acceptance of living in condemned property
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim character moment - his oblivious acceptance of terrible circumstances as mere 'inconveniences'

jokes.text: Jim immediately tries to sleep standing up in the garage
jokes.setup: Alex suggests Jim go get some sleep somewhere
jokes.punchline: Jim literally tries to sleep standing in the garage immediately
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim literal interpretation visual gag - takes Alex's suggestion completely literally

jokes.text: Good night, Alex. [Beat] Forget it, Iggy. You can't stay here.
jokes.setup: Alex suggests Jim go get some sleep
jokes.punchline: Jim immediately tries to sleep right there in the garage, Alex has to shut it down
jokes.explanation: Clean physical comedy beat showing Jim's literal-mindedness. Economical execution.

jokes.text: Well, sometimes I leave the cap off the toothpaste. I forget to wipe my feet, and I usually scream for several hours in my sleep.
jokes.setup: Asked if he'd be a good houseguest
jokes.punchline: Escalates from minor habits to horrifying sleep screaming
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - starts with relatable flaws, ends with nightmare scenario

jokes.text: It's you, boss.
jokes.setup: Jim asked to choose which friend he'd most want to stay with
jokes.punchline: Chooses Louie, the person who treats him worst
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and character work - Jim genuinely sees Louie as his best friend despite the abuse

jokes.text: If we didn't live in a society that got hung up on the fear of members of the same sex sharing mutual affection for each other, I'd kiss you full on the mouth.
jokes.setup: Jim expressing gratitude to Louie
jokes.punchline: Overly intellectual justification for wanting to kiss Louie
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim pseudo-intellectual rambling leading to uncomfortable conclusion

jokes.text: Why June 15? Okay, well, how about tonight?
jokes.setup: Latka invites everyone to dinner on specific date
jokes.punchline: When questioned about the date, immediately suggests tonight instead
jokes.explanation: Nice Latka character moment - his logic is endearingly arbitrary

jokes.text: I am married, Louie. Well, hey, congratulations! When did this happen? About 11 years ago.
jokes.setup: Louie giving Jeff marriage advice
jokes.punchline: Jeff has been married 11 years, showing Louie knows nothing about his employees
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie character moment - his complete ignorance about his employees' lives

jokes.text: The guy's screaming and sobbing so loud, I can't tell.
jokes.setup: Phone call for Alex
jokes.punchline: Jim is so hysterical on the phone he's unrecognizable
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the apartment disaster, shows Jim's extreme panic

jokes.text: Then it's noticeable!
jokes.setup: Alex sees the completely destroyed apartment
jokes.punchline: Massively understated reaction to total devastation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction to visual devastation - economy of words makes it funnier

jokes.text: Well, it's the old story. I was smoking in bed. And you fell asleep? No. I didn't notice the flames consuming the apartment.
jokes.setup: Smoking in bed suggests falling asleep with cigarette
jokes.punchline: Jim was awake but somehow didn't notice fire consuming apartment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jim logic - subverts expectations about smoking in bed accidents with his characteristic obliviousness

jokes.text: Do you think Louie's gonna be upset? The question is, is whether he'll kill you or not.
jokes.setup: Jim worries if Louie will be upset about the destruction
jokes.punchline: Alex escalates to murder as the real concern
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jim's continued obliviousness to the severity

jokes.text: Jim's manic renovation plans with paint, wallpaper, and strategically placed flowers
jokes.setup: Complete apartment destruction
jokes.punchline: Jim thinks flowers can fix fire damage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim delusion - thinking cosmetic fixes can address structural devastation

jokes.text: Jim's complete emotional breakdown and Alex trying to calm him
jokes.setup: Alex tells Jim not to panic
jokes.punchline: Jim immediately has complete meltdown
jokes.explanation: Standard panic comedy but works for character dynamics

jokes.text: Oh, Iggy, I'm home! What are you cooking?
jokes.setup: Louie arrives home cheerfully
jokes.punchline: He's asking about cooking when the apartment is completely burned
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - audience knows what Louie doesn't, innocent question becomes absurd

jokes.text: Just keep in mind that material possessions are simply excess baggage on the journey of life.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to soften the blow of destroying everything
jokes.punchline: Philosophical platitude about possessions being unimportant
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim pseudo-wisdom at the worst possible moment

jokes.text: I saved your most prized possession. See, boss? Your beloved violin.
jokes.setup: Jim heroically saved Louie's most precious item
jokes.punchline: It's a violin, setting up the reveal that Louie doesn't play
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the piano reveal - Jim's assumption about what's valuable

jokes.text: Good thing I didn't save the piano.
jokes.setup: Louie devastated that his violin was saved
jokes.punchline: Jim reveals he chose to save violin instead of piano
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - Jim's good intentions backfire spectacularly. Perfect economy and devastating logic.

jokes.text: My Time Life books on the great lizards.
jokes.setup: Louie listing his destroyed possessions
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific book collection about lizards
jokes.explanation: Great character detail - reveals Louie's unexpected interests through specific absurdity

jokes.text: My Time Life books on the great lizards.
jokes.setup: Louie listing his destroyed possessions
jokes.punchline: Reveals he owned an oddly specific and nerdy book collection
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail that's incongruous with Louie's tough guy image. The specificity makes it funny.

jokes.text: These two pool balls are okay.
jokes.setup: Louie mourning his destroyed bumper pool table
jokes.punchline: Jim cheerfully offers the only salvageable parts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim obliviousness - offering useless consolation with genuine helpfulness

jokes.text: I was a success here not because of the job, but because of that man over there. He yelled at me. He abused me, but he made me work!
jokes.setup: Jim explaining why he was successful at the job
jokes.punchline: Credits his success to Louie's abuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim psychology - genuinely appreciates what others would see as terrible treatment

jokes.text: Try Amityville.
jokes.setup: Jim asks where else he could find someone like Louie
jokes.punchline: Reference to famously haunted/evil location
jokes.explanation: Solid reference joke comparing Louie to supernatural evil, good economy

jokes.text: dictated but not read, James Ignatowski
jokes.setup: Formal letter closing
jokes.punchline: Jim admits he didn't even read his own apology letter
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail showing Jim's detachment from normal social conventions even in crisis.

jokes.text: A blank check? You can't give a blank check to Louie!
jokes.setup: Revelation that Jim's father sent a blank check
jokes.punchline: Alex's horror at giving Louie unlimited access to money
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the negotiation sequence, Alex as voice of reason

jokes.text: I'm salivating. Ha, I'm actually salivating.
jokes.setup: Louie seeing the blank check
jokes.punchline: Physical reaction of greed - literally drooling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie character moment - greed so intense it causes physical reaction

jokes.text: Does it mean you love me again? I didn't love you before.
jokes.setup: Jim asking if Louie loves him again
jokes.punchline: Louie's blunt clarification that he never loved him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - crushing Jim's romantic notions with brutal honesty

jokes.text: That's why you're a cabdriver, Reiger. If I fill this check out for a million bucks, his dad would never cover it. But there is a figure, an exact figure...
jokes.setup: Alex suggests asking for the real replacement cost
jokes.punchline: Louie's sophisticated scheme to maximize profit while ensuring payment
jokes.explanation: Great Louie character work - shows his cunning and greed in calculating the perfect scam amount

jokes.text: If I say 45, he'll say 46... So, we rule out the 40s.
jokes.setup: Louie's convoluted thought process about amount selection
jokes.punchline: His circular logic leads him to eliminate entire ranges of numbers
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Louie's neurotic overthinking, making greed unnecessarily complicated.

jokes.text: You know, in its own way, this is kind of spellbinding.
jokes.setup: Watching Louie's extended calculation process
jokes.punchline: Character acknowledges they're witnessing something bizarrely fascinating
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary acknowledging the absurdity of the extended sequence. Decent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: 27,000! 27! It's what I said the first time! 27. Tell him 27. 27,000. 29,000. 540.
jokes.setup: Louie trying to settle on the perfect amount
jokes.punchline: His decision-making process completely breaks down into incoherent number shouting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Louie's greed overwhelming his rationality. The breakdown is believable.

jokes.text: He said he was afraid it was gonna be something like $200,000.
jokes.setup: Dad was relieved about the amount
jokes.punchline: Dad expected something much higher, showing $29,000 was actually low
jokes.explanation: Perfect final twist - Louie's careful calculation was actually conservative, revealing his missed opportunity

jokes.text: Damn it! That kiss is gonna happen sometimes, and you know it.
jokes.setup: Jim's continued affection toward Louie
jokes.punchline: Louie accepting the inevitable romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Great callback and character development - Louie's resignation to Jim's affection creates unexpected intimacy
